entry.title: BIP 118 and SIGHASH_ANYPREVOUT
entry.summary:
The recent developments in Bitcoin Improvement Proposal 118 (BIP 118) have led to significant updates, primarily influenced by the integration of the ANYPREVOUT feature. The decision was made to update the existing BIP 118 rather than assigning a new BIP number. This choice reflects a streamlined approach to enhancements within the Bitcoin protocol, as detailed in the newly opened draft Pull Request on GitHub, which can be viewed [here](https://github.com/bitcoin/bips/pull/943).
The updated proposal, accessible for review [on GitHub](https://github.com/ajtowns/bips/blob/bip-anyprevout/bip-0118.mediawiki), removes previously considered elements such as chaperone signatures, indicating a shift towards simplification and perhaps broadening the application scope of the BIP without these additional complexities. Furthermore, the ANYPREVOUTANYSCRIPT component has been revised to no longer commit to the transaction value, an adjustment that likely impacts how transactions can be constructed and verified under this proposal.
This evolution in the BIP represents a clear move towards refining the technical mechanisms of Bitcoin transactions, focusing on enhancing flexibility and security in transaction handling without overcomplicating the user input requirements. The absence of tested code at this stage suggests that the proposal is primarily open for discussion, aiming to solidify its concepts with community feedback before final implementation.
